title: Live On! by TOUCH Community Services
text:
Live On! by TOUCH Community Services - SupportGoWhere
Live On! by TOUCH Community Services
Share link
Supports youths with high suicide risks as they transit from hospitalisation into the community with the support of the family.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based, Home-based
Free
Referral required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Live On! is a suicide intervention programme that targets youths aged 12 to 18 years old with suicidality, and includes their family members in the intervention.

Objectives of the programme includes:

Providing immediate support to youths with suicidal thoughts or attempts in the community

Providing continuous community support for youths and the families once they are discharged from primary care institutions

De-escalating youths' emotions and strengthening family support through individual and family counselling

Who is this for?

12 to 18 years old youths who have been discharged from IMH due to suicidality.

What to expect?

This programme consists of individual counselling for the youths, as well as family sessions.

Individual sessions (for the youth)

In the acute phase, the youth will meet with the social worker / counsellor / psychologist weekly for 6 to 12 sessions to de-escalate emotions

In the continuation phase, the youth will meet with the social worker / counsellor / psychologist twice a month, for another 5 to 12 sessions

After the above is completed, the youth will remain in contact with the caseworker through calls and text messages for 3 to 6 months for continuous support and monitoring

Family sessions (for the caregivers of the youth)

Meet with the social worker / counsellor twice a month from the start of the programme for 4 to 6 sessions to learn skills on how to support the youth better, which includes safety planning and crisis management

School counsellors will also be contacted, with client and parental consent.

How to apply?

This programme is only eligible for youths who have been discharged by IMH due to suicidality, and have been assessed by an IMH clinician to be eligible for referral.

Call 63892000 or visit IMH website for more information.

Information last updated on 29 Sep 2025
